description: A designed vertical share-card built in a Fox News style. At the top on a white band is the bold 'FOX NEWS' logo, followed by a large black serif headline: "Labor secretary announces 'strike team' going to Minnesota to investigate rampant fraud". A torn/ripped-paper edge separates the white headline area from the lower photographic half, which shows President Trump from the knees up at a campaign-style rally: he wears a dark navy suit, white shirt and purple patterned tie with an American-flag lapel pin, smiling and pointing directly at the viewer. Behind him is a blurred crowd in red and green clothing and a blue backdrop. A small white Department of Labor eagle/seal watermark sits in the lower-left corner, marking this as an agency-produced graphic. The composition repackages a friendly news headline as agency promotion. Propaganda technique: borrowed credibility from a news brand plus a 'strike team' / 'rampant fraud' framing to project tough enforcement, with the pointing-Trump image conveying decisive leadership. Target audience: the administration's base and general public, promoting DOL anti-fraud action.
notable_text: FOX NEWS; Labor secretary announces 'strike team' going to Minnesota to investigate rampant fraud
